documents.title: Qwen
documents.description: Qwen Studio offers comprehensive functionality spanning chatbot, image and video understanding, image generation, document processing, web search integration, tool utilization, and artifacts.
documents.summary: This document introduces Qwen Code, an open-source AI agent for the terminal designed to assist developers in understanding large codebases and automating tasks. It details its capabilities across various modes including interactive use, headless scripting, IDE integration, and providing a developer SDK.

It covers installation instructions, basic usage with codebase analysis, and advanced capabilities like debugging, navigation across entire projects, and automating tedious development workflows.

documents.title: Announcing Qwen Code: An AI Coding Agent That Thinks Like a Programmer
documents.description: Introducing Qwen Code, an open-source AI coding tool powered by the Qwen3-Coder model, ushering in a new era of Agentic Workflow.
documents.summary: This document introduces Qwen Code, an open-source, autonomous AI programming companion powered by Qwen3-Coder. It explains that Qwen Code can perform advanced coding tasks like debugging and refactoring across multiple environments (terminal, IDE, CI/CD, browser) and operates with agentic capabilities.

documents.title: One Sentence: Create a Blockbuster Promotional Video for Your Open Source Project
documents.description: Introducing Qwen Code's oss-video skill: just provide a GitHub repository URL, and it automatically generates a high-quality promotional video for your open source project
documents.summary:
This document introduces a new skill within Qwen Code that allows users to generate cinematic promotional demo videos for their open-source projects using only a single natural language prompt.
It details the process, use cases ranging from student assignments to corporate promotion, and emphasizes how this lowers the barrier for showcasing technical strength.

documents.title: I Used AI to Clean Up My Chaotic Desktop: Complete Guide to Qwen Code Cowork Use Cases
documents.description: A free and open-source AI work assistant that helps you organize files, batch rename, clean up storage space, and automate daily tasks.
documents.summary: This document introduces Qwen Code Cowork, a free and open-source desktop AI assistant capable of automating complex local tasks like file organization, batch renaming, managing disk space, and integrating diverse documents using natural language commands.

documents.title: Turns Out AI Knows Me Better Than I Know Myself—Experiencing Qwen Code Insight
documents.description: Sharing my experience with Qwen Code's new Insight feature, using data to show you how you use AI and how AI can guide you to use it better.
documents.summary: This document details the Qwen Code Insight feature, which analyzes a user's entire conversation history with the AI to generate personalized reports. These insights reveal usage patterns, pinpoint systematic pain points, and provide actionable recommendations for improvement.

documents.title: Qwen Code Beta: Use AI to Write Code in Your WeChat Channel!
documents.description: Attention, attention! This is not crayfish, this is Qwen Code's Channel feature! Fresh out of the oven
documents.summary: This document details how developers can utilize Qwen Code for coding assistance directly within WeChat channels, eliminating the need for traditional IDEs or terminals. It provides a comprehensive guide covering prerequisites, step-by-step configuration (connecting via QR code and editing settings), testing procedures, and advanced customization tips.

documents.title: Qwen Code + Obsidian: Making Knowledge Management Accessible
documents.description: Integrate Qwen Code into Obsidian to enhance knowledge management efficiency through plugin installation, authentication configuration, and multimodal capabilities like writing polish, image generation, and web scraping.

It covers everything from initial environment setup (installing plugins) to advanced workflows like file referencing, language modification, writing polish, image generation, and accessing protected web content.

documents.description: Use Qwen Code to quickly build personal websites, participate in open source projects, and generate demo videos. Practical guide for AI programming tools.
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive guide on leveraging the Qwen Code tool across various development tasks, demonstrating how it can automate creating portfolio websites, contributing to open-source projects via PR submission, and generating professional demo videos using code.

documents.title: A Letter to University Students: 10 Real Use Cases for Qwen Code

documents.title: Qwen Code Weekly: LSP Support, Batch Runner, New Languages
documents.description: This week we released v0.8.1, v0.8.2 stable versions and v0.9.0 preview, adding LSP support, batch-runner evaluation tool, unified UI architecture, and Japanese & Portuguese language support.
documents.summary: This weekly blog post details the latest updates to Qwen Code, highlighting major new features such as a comprehensive Extension System, native Language Server Protocol (LSP) support for advanced code intelligence, and a batch-runner tool for efficient testing. It also covers UI architecture unification and international language additions.

documents.title: Qwen Code Weekly: Agent Skills GA, One-Click Install, Session Export
documents.description: This week we released v0.9.0, v0.9.1 stable versions and v0.10.0 preview. Agent Skills removed experimental flag, added one-click install script and session history export feature.
documents.summary: This weekly blog post details updates for Qwen Code, announcing major stable feature releases like Agent Skills going General Availability and adding session history export capabilities across multiple formats. It also covers installation improvements, SDK optimizations, and numerous bug fixes.

documents.title: Qwen Code Weekly: Insight Analytics, Clipboard Images, Terminal Capture
documents.description: This week we released v0.10.0, v0.11.0 major feature versions and 5 bugfix versions, adding Insight analytics, clipboard image support, Terminal Capture and more.
documents.summary: This blog post details the latest updates and new features in Qwen Code, including built-in insight analytics, cross-platform clipboard image support, automated terminal screenshot generation, and various bug fixes across different versions.

documents.title: Qwen Code Weekly: HTML Export Enhancement, Terminal GIF Recording, GitHub Workflow Commands
documents.description: This week we released v0.11.1 and continued development on v0.12.0, adding an HTML export tool call viewer, terminal streaming GIF recording, built-in GitHub workflow commands, and more.
documents.summary: This blog post details the latest enhancements and stability fixes in a software release, highlighting new features like improved HTML exports, streaming terminal GIF generation, built-in GitHub workflow commands, and various quality-of-life improvements.

documents.title: Qwen Code Weekly: Automated Workflows, Better Extension & MCP Management, VS Code Sidebar
documents.description: This week's update lets you automate script execution, manage MCP and extensions more easily, keep Qwen Code in VS Code sidebar, and generate GIFs from screen recordings with one click.
documents.summary: This release announcement details several major updates to Qwen Code v0.12.0, introducing features like an automated Hooks system, proactive AI questioning capabilities, and enhanced visual interfaces for extension and MCP management.

documents.title: Qwen Code Weekly: Token Limit Doubled, Real-time Usage Display, JetBrains Editor Support
documents.description: This week: Token limit increased from 8K to 16K, real-time token usage display, JetBrains/Zed editor integration, Plan Mode comparison view, plus Windows encoding fixes.
documents.summary: This blog post summarizes the latest features and bug fixes released for Qwen Code, highlighting key updates such as doubling the token limit to 16K, real-time token usage display, integration with JetBrains and Zed editors, and general stability improvements across various platforms.

documents.title: Qwen Code Weekly: AI Code Review, Ask Questions Without Losing Context, Custom Workflow Automation
documents.description: This week: v0.13.0 released with /review for code review before commits, /btw for quick questions without losing context, Hooks for automated testing and formatting, Arena for multi-model comparison.
documents.summary: This release document details the new features in Qwen Code v0.13.0, focusing on making it more programmable through hooks, skills sharing, and enhanced workflow integrations like code review and side questioning.
